name: White cabbage (food medium)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Moisture of raw cabbage.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 169975; Rosa et al. 2001 J Sci Food Agric doi:10.1002/jsfa.919
components.evidence: Glucose is the dominant free sugar in white cabbage.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 169975; Rosa et al. 2001 doi:10.1002/jsfa.919
components.evidence: Second most abundant free sugar.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor disaccharide (~11% of total sugars).

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Trace.

components.name: L-malic acid
components.source: Fresh/fermenting white cabbage organic-acid study (ResearchGate fig tbl2, doi:10.1007/... ; total fresh acidity ~0.4%); sauerkraut baseline reviews
components.evidence: Malic acid is the predominant organic acid of fresh cabbage; midpoint of reported ~150-300 mg/100 g. A dominant fermentable carbon/energy source (malolactic substrate).

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Fresh white cabbage organic-acid literature; declines during fermentation
components.evidence: Second organic acid after malic; ~50-100 mg/100 g range, midpoint used.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Oxalate content reviews (cabbage classed low-oxalate, <10 mg/100 g fw)
components.evidence: Low-oxalate Brassica; midpoint ~7 mg/100 g (range 4-10, some studies higher).

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Present as a minor organic acid, not reliably quantified for fresh white cabbage.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Trace, present but unquantified.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Total crude protein; amino-acid profile below is protein-derived.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Protein-bound + free.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (incl. glutamine); free glutamate contributes umami/N source.

components.name: L-glutamine
components.evidence: Cabbage is rich in free glutamine; not individually quantified by USDA (counted within glutamate).

components.name: L-citrulline
components.evidence: Free non-protein amino acid present; unquantified.

components.name: gamma-aminobutyrate (GABA)
components.evidence: Present as free amino acid; unquantified here.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Cabbage is a very low-fat matrix.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Small ALA fraction; dominant PUFA in leaf lipids.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: High vitamin C; note lower 13-19 mg/100g in some cultivar surveys (PMC7464038).

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Not in USDA panel; approximate.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Total folate.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Total choline.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin A carotenoid (vitamin A ~5 ug RAE).

components.name: phylloquinone (vitamin K1)
components.evidence: Notably high for a pale leaf vegetable.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: As phosphate; key P source.

components.name: sulfur
components.evidence: Cabbage is sulfur-rich (glucosinolates, S-methylmethionine); total S not in USDA panel.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates FooDB 'Iron', 'Iron (Fe3+)', 'Fe2+ mitochondria' duplicates.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Present counter-ion; unquantified in USDA panel.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic contaminant element, not a nutrient

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element (K analogue), not a defined nutrient

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: ultratrace; not a growth substrate for microbes in this matrix

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal metabolite, biologically implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Aniline
remove.reason: not a food nutrient; predicted/contaminant amine, implausible as a growth substrate

remove.name: N-Methylaniline
remove.reason: predicted/contaminant amine, not a genuine cabbage nutrient

remove.name: N-Methylphenethylamine
remove.reason: predicted trace amine, not a defined growth nutrient

remove.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
remove.reason: oxidation artifact of methionine, not a distinct nutrient

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: gas placeholder, not a compositional nutrient of the food

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: gas placeholder, not a food nutrient

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: proton placeholder, not a compositional nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: ultratrace cobalt placeholder; not growth-relevant and not quantified

missing_added: L-malic acid (dominant organic acid, was absent)
succinic acid, fumaric acid (present but unquantified)
individual free sugars with amounts (glucose > fructose >> sucrose)
consolidated iron and removed organelle/oxidation-state duplicates
sources: Rosa et al. 2001, J Sci Food Agric, doi:10.1002/jsfa.919 (glucose/fructose/sucrose in white cabbage)
PMC7464038 Comparative Evaluation of Bioactive Compounds and Volatile Profile of White Cabbages (sugars, ascorbate, sinapic acid)
Fresh/fermenting white cabbage organic-acid study (ResearchGate fig tbl2 id 338031753; fresh total acidity ~0.4%, malic/citric predominant)
Oxalate-content reviews classifying cabbage as low-oxalate (<10 mg/100 g fw)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for growth: free glucose (~1.7 g) and fructose (~1.4 g), plus L-malic acid (~0.24 g) as the principal organic acid and a malolactic substrate; citric acid secondary. Nitrogen supplied largely by free glutamate/glutamine and a full amino-acid pool. Very low fat. Organic-acid amounts are literature midpoints (medium confidence); succinic/fumaric present but unquantified. FooDB noise (heavy metals, rare-earths, animal metabolites, predicted amines, gas placeholders) removed.
